bond.definition: A strong feeling of closeness and connection between people.
bond.example: The bond between parent and child grows over time.

throughout.definition: During the whole time of something.
throughout.example: She smiled throughout the entire performance.

environment.definition: The world around you; the conditions you live in.
environment.example: A calm environment helps babies feel safe.

surround.definition: To be all around something or someone.

instant.example: She felt an instant connection with the baby.

connection.definition: A feeling of being close to and understanding someone.
connection.example: The mother felt a strong connection with her newborn.

fiercely.definition: With very strong feeling or intensity.

stool.definition: A seat without a back; a 'birthing stool' is a special seat used during birth.
stool.example: She used a birthing stool during labor.

contractions.definition: The tightening movements of the muscles in the womb during birth.
contractions.example: The contractions came every five minutes.

waves.definition: Things that come and go in a regular pattern, like ocean waves.
waves.example: The pain came in waves, getting stronger each time.

gas.definition: Here, a pain-reducing gas breathed in during birth (nitrous oxide).
gas.example: She used gas and air to manage the pain during delivery.

idolizing.definition: Loving and admiring someone very much, almost too much.
idolizing.example: New parents cannot help idolizing their own baby.

pitying.example: She was pitying the other mothers whose babies were not as cute.

oxytocin.definition: A natural chemical in the body that creates feelings of love and bonding.
oxytocin.example: Oxytocin is released when a mother holds her baby.

hormone.definition: A chemical made by the body that controls how you feel and how your body works.
hormone.example: Hormones can affect your mood during pregnancy.

release.definition: The act of letting something go or flow out.
release.example: The release of oxytocin helps mothers bond with babies.

induced.definition: When birth is started by medicine rather than happening naturally.
induced.example: Her baby was induced because it was two weeks late.

extremely.definition: To a very high degree; very much.

epidural.definition: A medical injection in the back that removes pain during birth.
epidural.example: She asked for an epidural to reduce the pain.

anaesthetist.definition: A doctor who gives medicine to stop pain, especially during surgery.
anaesthetist.example: The anaesthetist prepared the injection for the patient.

shock.definition: A sudden feeling of surprise and upset; being unable to react normally.
shock.example: She was in shock after hearing the news.

difficulty.definition: A problem; something that is hard to deal with.
difficulty.example: She had difficulty sleeping after the baby was born.

DNA.definition: The material inside cells that carries information about what you look like; used for identifying family connections.
DNA.example: A DNA test can prove if two people are related.

unit.definition: A special department or section in a hospital.
unit.example: The baby was taken to the special-care unit.

gradually.definition: Slowly, step by step, over time.
gradually.example: She gradually started to feel better.

cemented.definition: Made strong and permanent; firmly established.
cemented.example: Their friendship was cemented by years of trust.

fist.definition: A hand with the fingers closed tightly together.
fist.example: The baby was hitting the water with his fist.

freak.definition: A person who seems strange or different from others (often used about yourself when feeling abnormal).
freak.example: She felt like a freak for not bonding with her baby immediately.

berate.definition: To criticize or scold someone harshly.
berate.example: Do not berate yourself for making a mistake.

acknowledged.definition: Recognized and accepted as real or true.
acknowledged.example: Her mother acknowledged her feelings without judging.

progress.definition: Moving forward; getting better over time.
progress.example: She made good progress in forming a bond with her baby.

exercise.definition: An activity done to practice or learn something.
exercise.example: Try this exercise to understand how your baby feels.

visualize.definition: To form a picture in your mind; to imagine.
visualize.example: Close your eyes and visualize a peaceful place.

uncomfortable.definition: Not feeling relaxed or at ease; slightly painful or awkward.
uncomfortable.example: Lying on the hard floor was uncomfortable.

communicate.definition: To share information or feelings with someone.
communicate.example: Babies communicate by crying and making sounds.

rescued.definition: Saved from a bad or dangerous situation.
rescued.example: The child felt rescued when her mother picked her up.

belong.definition: To feel accepted and part of a group.
belong.example: Everyone wants to feel they belong somewhere.
